document.source_title: WHO calls for action: “Together for health. Stand with science.” to mark World Health Day

claims.claim_text: The global maternal mortality rate has fallen by more than 40% since 2000.
claims.evidence_text: The global maternal mortality rate has fallen by more than 40% since 2000.

claims.claim_text: Deaths among children under five have been reduced by over 50%.
claims.evidence_text: Deaths among children under five have been reduced by over 50%.

claims.claim_text: Over the past 50 years, global immunization efforts have saved over 154 million children from infectious diseases.
claims.evidence_text: Over the past 50 years, global immunization efforts have saved over 154 million children from infectious diseases.

entities.name: World Health Organization
entities.description: The World Health Organization is a specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for international public health.
entities.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) today calls on people everywhere to renew their commitment to working together and supporting science.

entities.name: One Health Summit
entities.description: A summit convened by WHO and the G7 Presidency of France to strengthen coordinated action for health.
entities.evidence_text: WHO and the G7 Presidency of France are convening a One Health Summit in Lyon, France, from 5–7 April.

events.evidence_text: WHO and the G7 Presidency of France are convening a One Health Summit in Lyon, France, from 5–7 April.
WHO will host the Global Forum of its Collaborating Centres network from 7–9 April.
